Afghanistan.notes: Afghanistan citizens enter their own country without a visa.

Albania.notes: E-visa (Type C) available online via e-visa.al; permits 90 days within 180-day period.

Algeria.notes: Afghan citizens must obtain a tourist visa from an Algerian embassy before arrival; maximum stay is 90 days.

Andorra.notes: Andorra imposes no visa requirement; however, a Schengen visa is required to access Andorra via France or Spain.

Angola.notes: Afghanistan is not eligible for Angola's online e-visa; tourist visa must be applied through traditional visa channels at Angolan embassy, valid for 30 days.

Antigua and Barbuda.notes: Afghanistan passport holders must obtain an Electronic Entry Visa (EEV) online; valid for 180 days with permitted stay up to 90 days per entry.

Argentina.notes: Afghanistan nationals must apply for a tourist visa in advance; maximum stay 90 days, renewable once for another 90 days.

Armenia.notes: Afghanistan citizens must apply for visa at Armenian diplomatic representations or consular posts abroad with an invitation only.

Australia.notes: Afghan citizens require a Visitor Visa (subclass 600); approved for 3, 6, or 12 months depending on circumstances.

Austria.notes: Schengen visa C required; apply in advance at Austrian consulate for up to 90 days stay.

Azerbaijan.notes: Afghanistan citizens must apply for a visa through the embassy of Azerbaijan; not eligible for visa-free, visa-on-arrival, or e-visa.

Bahamas.notes: Afghan nationals must obtain a Bahamian visitor visa through the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in advance; not eligible for eVisa.

Bahrain.notes: E-visa required; apply online at evisa.gov.bh before travel. Maximum stay 30 days after arrival.

Bangladesh.notes: Afghan nationals must obtain a tourist visa in advance; single-entry visa valid for 3 months.

Barbados.notes: Afghan nationals must obtain a visa in advance; single-entry visa valid for 3 months, multiple-entry for 6 months.

Belarus.notes: Afghan citizens require a visa applied in advance at a Belarusian embassy or consulate; tourist visas issued for up to 30 days.

Belgium.notes: Afghan nationals must apply at the Belgian Embassy in Islamabad through a Visa Application Centre; processing requires minimum 3 weeks.

Belize.notes: Afghanistan passport holders require a visa issued by Belize Department of Immigration; maximum 30-day stay.

Benin.notes: E-visa required; apply online via evisa.bj between 7–90 days before travel; valid 30 or 90 days depending on type.

Bhutan.notes: Afghanistan passport holders must apply for an e-visa online through Bhutan's immigration system; visa approval typically takes 5 working days.

Bolivia.notes: Group 3 citizens require visa authorization from Bolivia's National Migration Service; tourist visa permits 30-day stay, extendable twice for same duration.

Bosnia and Herzegovina.notes: Type C visa required; 90 days max within 180-day period; apply at embassy/consulate in advance.

Botswana.notes: E-visa required; apply online at the official Botswana e-visa portal. Maximum stay is 90 days within a 12-month period.

Brazil.notes: VIVIS (visitor visa) required; in-person embassy application; maximum 90-day stay for tourism.

Brunei.notes: Afghanistan classified as Category A; visa required, applied through Brunei diplomatic missions.

Bulgaria.notes: Afghanistan passport holders require a Schengen visa; apply at Bulgarian embassy in New Delhi, Islamabad, or Tehran.

Burkina Faso.notes: Afghan nationals require an eVisa; tourist visa valid for up to 90 days, available through the official Burkina Faso eVisa portal (launched August 2023).

Burundi.notes: Non-visa-exempt countries including Afghanistan can obtain a 30-day visa on arrival at Bujumbura Airport for $90 USD.

Cambodia.notes: Afghanistan nationals require an e-Visa for Cambodia tourism; apply online, single entry, max 30 days stay.

Cameroon.notes: Afghan nationals can apply online for a short-stay e-visa for tourism; valid for up to 180 days.

Canada.notes: Afghan passport holders require a Temporary Resident Visa to visit Canada; must apply in advance at a Canadian visa office.

Cape Verde.notes: Visa required in advance from Cape Verdean embassy or consulate; visa-on-arrival suspended January 1, 2026 under Decree No. 244/GMAI/2025.

Central African Republic.notes: Visa required, must be obtained in advance through CAR Embassy in Paris; yellow fever vaccination required.

Chad.notes: Afghan citizens can apply for an electronic visa through Chad's official e-visa system launched December 11, 2024; valid for 90 days.

Chile.notes: SERMIG indicates some countries require prior consulate authorization; Afghanistan appears to be in exception list but consulado.gob.cl dropdown required for confirmation.

China.notes: Afghan citizens require an L visa (tourist visa) obtained from the Chinese Embassy in Afghanistan before travel.

Colombia.notes: Afghan citizens must apply for an online eVisa (electronic visitor visa) through the Colombian Ministry of Foreign Affairs portal; initial stay up to 90 days, extendable for an additional 90 days.

Comoros.notes: Visa on arrival available for all nationalities at entry ports; costs 30-50 EUR; valid for 45 days maximum.

Congo.notes: Afghan citizens must apply for an eVisa online; valid 90 days from issue with maximum 7-day stay.

Costa Rica.notes: Afghan citizens require visa; Fourth Group classification; rarely granted unless minor, disabled, or family reunification; minimum 90-day processing.

Croatia.notes: Afghan nationals must apply for a Schengen visa through a Croatian embassy or consulate; maximum 90 days in any 180-day period.

Cuba.notes: Afghan citizens require A-1 Tourist Visa with consular approval; e-visa not available for this nationality.

Cyprus.notes: Tourist visa must be applied for at a Cyprus embassy or consulate in advance.

Czech Republic.notes: Afghan nationals require Schengen visa (short-term) obtained in advance through Czech Embassy in Islamabad; allows stay up to 90 days within 180-day period.

DR Congo.notes: Afghan nationals eligible for single-entry e-visa, valid for 7 days within 3 months of issue.

Denmark.notes: Visa required via embassy; Afghanistan classified as Group 5 (high immigration risk), tourist visas rarely granted except for extenuating circumstances.

Djibouti.notes: Afghanistan nationals eligible for Djibouti eVisa; online application required, valid passport 6+ months, max 30-day stay.

Dominica.notes: Afghanistan passport holders are not exempt and must obtain a visa before travel; contact Dominica Labour Division for application details.

Dominican Republic.notes: Tourist visa required; issued for 60 days validity with maximum initial stay of 30 days.

Ecuador.notes: Afghanistan requires an eVisa; valid for 90 days with 30-day maximum stay; $50 USD online at serviciosdigitales.cancilleria.gob.ec

Egypt.notes: Afghan nationals are not eligible for e-visa and must apply for a traditional tourist visa through the Egyptian Embassy with 6-8 week minimum processing time.

El Salvador.notes: Visa required; must apply at Salvadoran embassy or consulate for approval before travel.

Equatorial Guinea.notes: E-visa required before arrival; must provide yellow fever vaccination certificate; processing takes up to 72 hours.

Eritrea.notes: Visa required; must apply in advance at Eritrean Embassy; tourist visa permits 30 days of stay.

Estonia.notes: Afghanistan nationals are not visa-exempt; a Schengen visa must be obtained in advance from an Estonian embassy or consulate.

Ethiopia.notes: Afghan nationals can apply for Ethiopian e-visa online via evisa.gov.et; tourist visas valid for 30 or 90 days depending on type.

Fiji.notes: Tourist visa required; apply at nearest Fiji embassy before travel. Maximum 4 months stay.

Finland.notes: Afghanistan nationals must apply for a Schengen visa in advance at the Finnish Embassy or VFS Global center; short stays limited to 90 days.

France.notes: Afghan citizens must obtain a Schengen visa through the Embassy of France in Tehran or Delhi before travel; valid for 3 months (90 days) maximum stay.

Gabon.notes: Afghanistan nationals require an electronic visa (e-Visa), applied online and issued within 72 hours.

Gambia.notes: Afghan nationals can obtain visa at Gambian embassies or at Banjul International Airport upon arrival (VOA).

Georgia.notes: Afghan citizens must apply for a visa through Georgian diplomatic missions unless they hold a valid GCC residence permit (UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman) for at least one year.

Germany.notes: Afghanistan nationals require a Schengen Visa for entry to Germany; apply at German embassy (Kabul closed; use Islamabad, Dubai, or Istanbul).

Ghana.notes: Afghanistan passport holders require a visa issued by the Ghanaian Embassy; not eligible for visa-free, on-arrival, or e-visa options.

Greece.notes: Type C Schengen visa required; must apply in person at Greek consulate or designated visa center

Grenada.notes: Afghanistan nationals require a prior-approval tourist visa from the Embassy of Grenada.

Guatemala.notes: Afghanistan passport holders require a tourist visa obtained from Guatemalan embassy/consulate before travel; maximum 90-day stay allowed.

Guinea.notes: Electronic visa required; apply online at official Guinea visa portal. Tourist visa valid up to 90 days.

Guinea-Bissau.notes: Visa on arrival available; maximum stay 90 days. Requires valid passport (6+ months validity) and authorization letter from Guinea-Bissau Migration Office.

Guyana.notes: Visa on arrival available through e-Services portal with Ministry of Home Affairs approval letter; tourist visa valid for 30 days.

Haiti.notes: Afghanistan passport holders are visa-free for up to 90 days; must pay $10 tourist fee upon arrival.

Honduras.notes: No directly accessible official Honduras government source found to confirm Afghanistan's visa status.

Hong Kong.notes: Afghanistan nationals must apply for a visit visa at the nearest Chinese diplomatic/consular mission or HKSAR Immigration Department before travel; standard processing takes 4 weeks.

Hungary.notes: Afghanistan citizens require a C-type Schengen visa; apply in advance at the Embassy of Hungary in Islamabad.

Iceland.notes: Schengen visa required; apply at Icelandic embassy in advance.

India.notes: Afghanistan nationals apply for the special e-Emergency X-Misc. Visa online; 30-day tourist e-visa available with double entry.

Indonesia.notes: Afghan nationals require calling visa; must apply online at evisa.imigrasi.go.id with Indonesian sponsor.

Iran.notes: Afghan passport holders must apply for visa at Iranian embassy or consulate in person; visa-on-arrival not available.

Iraq.notes: Afghan citizens excluded from e-Visa system; must apply through Iraqi embassy or consulate for traditional visa.

Ireland.notes: Afghan nationals must apply for a short stay C tourist visa through an Irish embassy in advance; no VFS office in Afghanistan, apply via Embassy of Ireland Abu Dhabi.

Israel.notes: Visa required; must apply at Israeli consulate with approximately 45 days processing time from appointment.

Italy.notes: Afghanistan nationals require a Schengen visa; maximum 90 days in any 180-day period for tourism.

Ivory Coast.notes: Visa required in advance at Ivorian embassy; Afghanistan not eligible for e-visa.

Jamaica.notes: Afghanistan nationals require an entry visa obtained prior to arrival at a Jamaican consular post.

Japan.notes: Afghan citizens must apply for a short-term visa at designated Japanese embassies/consulates outside Afghanistan (UAE, Pakistan, Iran, Turkey); not eligible for Japan eVisa.

Jordan.notes: Afghanistan is a restricted country; must obtain prior Ministry of Interior approval in advance; cannot use e-Visa or visa-on-arrival.

Kazakhstan.notes: Afghan nationals must apply for tourist visa (B12) at Kazakhstan embassy/consulate in advance; ineligible for e-visa system.

Kenya.notes: Afghan nationals require a referred visa approved by Director of Immigration Services; must apply at least 4 weeks in advance.

Kiribati.notes: Afghan citizens must apply for a visitor visa; single-entry visas valid up to 3 months with max 90 days per 12-month period.

Kosovo.notes: Afghanistan is not on Kosovo's visa exemption list; tourists must apply for a visa at a Kosovo embassy or consulate in advance.

Kuwait.notes: Kuwait maintains a security-based visa ban on Afghanistan nationals; no tourist visa can be obtained.

Kyrgyzstan.notes: Electronic tourist visa required; single entry valid for up to 60 days.

Laos.notes: Afghan nationals must obtain visas at Lao embassies/consulates in advance; not eligible for visa on arrival or e-visa.

Latvia.notes: Schengen short-stay visa required; apply directly at Embassy of Latvia in Uzbekistan with questionnaire and appointments by email.

Lebanon.notes: Visa required; Afghan nationals must apply through Lebanese embassy abroad with passport (6+ months valid), hotel reservation, airline ticket, and proof of funds.

Lesotho.notes: Afghan citizens must obtain a visa in advance from the Ministry of Home Affairs; processing time up to 10 working days.

Libya.notes: Afghanistan is ineligible for Libya's eVisa; must apply through embassy/consulate for traditional visa. Tourist visa allows 30-day stay.

Liechtenstein.notes: Schengen visa required for short stays (up to 90 days per 180-day period); apply at German Embassy.

Lithuania.notes: Schengen visa required; apply at Lithuanian embassy (New Delhi handles Afghanistan).

Luxembourg.notes: Schengen visa required; maximum 90 days within 180-day period; apply at Luxembourg embassy in advance.

Macao.notes: Visa on arrival available at border checkpoints; maximum stay 30 days.

Madagascar.notes: E-visa required; apply online through Madagascar's official government portal before travel. All nationalities eligible.

Malawi.notes: Afghanistan is Category 1; e-visa required before travel; tourist visa valid for max 30 days single entry.

Malaysia.notes: Afghanistan nationals require an eVisa; apply online and allow 3-5 working days for approval.

Maldives.notes: Visa on arrival; requires Yellow Fever vaccination, valid passport (1+ month), travel itinerary, and Traveller Declaration via IMUGA within 96 hours.

Mali.notes: Afghanistan citizens require a traditional visa obtained in advance from a Mali embassy or consulate; tourists typically receive 30–90 days depending on application.

Malta.notes: Schengen visa required; apply at the nearest Maltese diplomatic mission or partner country before travel.

Marshall Islands.notes: Visitor Visa (V-1) required; must apply in advance at embassy ($100 USD fee).

Mauritania.notes: Electronic visa required before travel; visa on arrival no longer available as of January 5, 2025.

Mauritius.notes: Visa required; apply in advance. Maximum 6 months per calendar year granted case-by-case by immigration officers at entry.

Mexico.notes: Afghan nationals must obtain a visa before traveling to Mexico; maximum 180-day tourist permit may be granted at immigration discretion.

Micronesia.notes: Valid passport, sufficient funds, and proof of onward travel required for 30-day visa-free entry.

Moldova.notes: Afghan citizens can apply for a Moldova electronic visa online through the official government portal, allowing single-entry stays of up to 90 days.

Monaco.notes: Afghanistan nationals require a Schengen visa to enter Monaco; application must be made at French consular authorities as Monaco uses Schengen visa system.

Mongolia.notes: Afghan nationals must apply for a K2 tourist visa at a Mongolian diplomatic mission; valid for up to 30 days.

Montenegro.notes: Afghan passport holders must obtain a visa from a Montenegrin diplomatic mission in advance; not visa-exempt.

Morocco.notes: Afghan passport holders require eVisa with Ministry of Interior approval; automatic processing not guaranteed.

Mozambique.notes: Tourism visa required; must apply in advance with confirmed flight/hotel, processed in 10 days ($220 USD).

Australia.notes: Australia citizens enter their own country without a visa.

Canada.notes: Australian citizens are visa-exempt and require an Electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) to fly to Canada; eTA costs CAD $7 and is valid for up to 5 years.

France.notes: Australian citizens are authorized to enter and stay in France without a visa for 90 days within any 180-day period under Schengen rules.

Germany.notes: Australian citizens are visa-free for up to 90 days in a 180-day period under a bilateral visa exemption agreement.

Greece.notes: Australian passport holders may enter Greece visa-free for tourism purposes for 90 days within any 6-month period.

Mexico.notes: Australian passport holders can enter Mexico visa-free for up to 180 days for tourism purposes with no advance visa application required.

Netherlands.notes: Australian passport holders are visa-free for the Netherlands and can stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period within the Schengen area.

Singapore.notes: Australian passport holders do not require a visa for tourist visits; they receive a Visit Pass upon arrival valid for up to 90 days.
